question: Operators flagged a short window of unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days. First identify the sensor channels installed on this asset, then run an anomaly check and trend analysis on the winding temperature channel (winding_temp_top_c) to determine whether the deviations form a single burst or a sustained shift.
effective_question: Operators flagged a short window of unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days. First identify the sensor channels installed on this asset, then run an anomaly check and trend analysis on the winding temperature channel (winding_temp_top_c) to determine whether the deviations form a single burst or a sustained shift. What is the typical operating range for the winding temperature on T-014? Are there any recent maintenance or repair activities on T-014 that could be contributing to the unusual readings?

clarifying_questions: What is the typical operating range for the winding temperature on T-014?
Are there any recent maintenance or repair activities on T-014 that could be contributing to the unusual readings?
assumptions: The sensor channels installed on T-014 are functioning correctly.
The anomaly check and trend analysis will be performed using historical data from the last few days.

answer: Based on the results, the unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days appear to be a single burst of anomalies rather than a sustained shift. The anomaly check identified two instances with high z-scores, and the trend analysis showed a decreasing trend with a slope of -1.754849°C per day, indicating a temporary deviation from the normal operating range.
